assets.name: lecture_07_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 is the clearest and least occluded frame. It preserves the central matrix-style board layout, the diagonal zeros, and the labeled component formula under C_3 with less interference from Susskind's hand or body than the earlier frames. It is the best single frame for reconstructing the lecture's point that the cross product can be written as an antisymmetric tensor.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The anti-symmetric tensor is one form of the cross product,

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 02 gives the clearest view of the three component formulas while keeping Susskind low enough in the frame not to interfere with the writing. The board content is already finished here, and the vertical list of component identities is more useful for note writing than the more gestural neighboring frames.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: forth between vector and anti-symmetric tensor.

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 02 gives the most complete mathematical view in this window. It preserves the left-hand trial force term built from F^{\mu\nu} and u_\nu while also keeping the right-hand contraction argument visible, where the antisymmetry leads to a vanishing scalar. The neighboring frames either hide more of the left equation or lose the right-side consequence.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: is to build the force out of an anti-symmetric tensor.
assets.caption_hint: Force ansatz from an antisymmetric tensor
